title: Tour de France 2025 — Data Modelling & Tableau Dashboards
description: End-to-end DB project for the Tour de France 2025. ER diagram → normalized 7-table PostgreSQL schema (Country, Location, Stage, Team, Rider, Participation, Exit) → seven Tableau dashboards for OLAP-style exploration covering rider/team performance, speed-by-stage-type, bonus/penalty counterfactuals, and national-level outcomes (Slovenia, Belgium, Denmark dominate the podium). Closing analysis benchmarks the normalized schema against a star-schema variant for analytics speed.

title: Student Depression Classification
description: Cost-sensitive Gradient Boosting classifier for early identification of at-risk students. Compared glass-box (logistic regression, decision tree) and black-box (gradient boosting, random forest) models under both standard L2 and an alternative surrogate classification loss, then tuned the decision threshold by minimizing a cost-sensitive evaluation loss that penalizes missed depressed students. Achieved 98% recall on the held-out set; SHAP for global and local interpretability.

title: Portfolio Backtesting — LASSO & Ridge
description: Used a portfolio-optimization framing to study overfitting in linear regression on high-dimensional financial data (100 daily-asset portfolios). Implemented Markowitz minimum-variance allocation, then re-cast it as a regression problem and compared LASSO and Ridge regularization in a rolling-window backtest (126-day train, 1-day test) to quantify the out-of-sample penalty of overfitting an unregularized covariance estimate.

title: PizzaHub Pricing Strategy — IV / 2SLS
description: Estimated regional demand functions for a food-delivery business using instrumental variables and two-stage least squares to isolate price elasticity from confounded discount campaigns. Validated OLS assumptions with Hausman tests and a battery of robustness checks, then translated the estimated elasticities into profit-maximizing pricing recommendations for two test regions.

title: Reflective Multi-Agent Work Reporting System
description: Multi-agent system where specialised agents (collector, summarizer, critic, writer) draft and refine a weekly work report, then a reflection agent critiques the draft and triggers a re-write loop until quality criteria are met. Tests how well closed-loop self-critique improves output quality on a real productivity task.
